definition: Earnings quality screening provides amplified downside protection (3-5x) under positive correlation, functioning as a primary hedge rather than merely an alpha source. The correlation regime systematically signals transition from growth-rewarded to cash-conversion-rewarded markets.
evidence: 2022: S&P 500 Quality Index sleeve drew down ~-11% vs standard ~-16% vs ARK-style ~-45%
Quality spread ~34pp in 2022 vs typical ~5-8pp in normal years (4-5x amplification)
Pair_1 debate: 'most actionable portfolio construction insight'
open_questions: Whether the 3-5x amplification is specific to 2022 or generalizable across positive correlation episodes
Systematic backtesting across full 1960s-2020s correlation history needed
